---
---

![expdpy — A Python library to explore panel data interactively](images/hero.webp){fig-align="center" fig-alt="expdpy — A Python library to explore panel data interactively"}

**expdpy** is a Python port of the [ExPanDaR](https://github.com/trr266/ExPanDaR) R package
(Joachim Gassen, TRR 266). It gives you a set of composable analytical functions and an
interactive web app for exploratory analysis of panel and cross-sectional data, built on the
modern Python stack:

- **[Plotly](https://plotly.com/python/)** — interactive figures
- **[pyfixest](https://github.com/py-econometrics/pyfixest)** — fixed-effects / clustered regressions
- **[Great Tables](https://posit-dev.github.io/great-tables/)** — publication-quality tables
- **[Shiny for Python](https://shiny.posit.co/py/)** and **[Streamlit](https://streamlit.io/)** — two no-code `ExPdPy` apps

There are **two interactive apps** with the same features: the original
[Shiny app](shiny.qmd) and a [Streamlit app](streamlit.qmd)
that adds a multipage layout and runs on [Streamlit Community Cloud](https://streamlit.io/cloud).
See [Shiny vs Streamlit](explanation/shiny-vs-streamlit.qmd) for a side-by-side comparison.

## Installation

The package is not on PyPI yet — install the latest version straight from GitHub:

```bash
# Core analytical functions:
pip install "git+https://github.com/cmg777/expdpy.git"

# ...with the interactive ExPdPy app:
pip install "expdpy[app] @ git+https://github.com/cmg777/expdpy.git"
```

Using [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/):

```bash
uv pip install "git+https://github.com/cmg777/expdpy.git"
uv pip install "expdpy[app] @ git+https://github.com/cmg777/expdpy.git"
```

Pin to a branch, tag, or commit for reproducible installs:

```bash
pip install "git+https://github.com/cmg777/expdpy.git@main"
# pip install "git+https://github.com/cmg777/expdpy.git@v0.1.0"   # once a release is tagged
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Coming soon (PyPI):** once published, `pip install expdpy` /
`pip install "expdpy[app]"` will work directly.
:::

## At a glance

The lead example throughout these docs is the bundled `kuznets` panel (80 countries ×
2015–2025): a synthetic dataset, rich in control variables, whose regional inequality traces
an **N-shaped Kuznets curve** in income — it rises, falls, then rises again at very high
income.

```python
import expdpy as ex
from expdpy.data import load_kuznets

df = load_kuznets()
# The N-shaped regional Kuznets curve: regional inequality vs (log) GDP per capita
ex.prepare_scatter_plot(
    df, x="log_gdp_pc", y="gini_regional", color="continent", size="population", loess=1
)
```

Head to the [Quickstart](quickstart.qmd) to see every function in action, the
[kuznets dataset](explanation/kuznets-dataset.qmd) page for the data dictionary, or the
[ExPdPy tutorial](shiny.qmd) to launch the interactive app.

## Why a port?

ExPanDaR is a wonderful tool for assessing the robustness of empirical evidence without
sharing the underlying data. `expdpy` brings the same workflow to Python users and
integrates with the `pandas` / `pyfixest` ecosystem. See
[Parity with ExPanDaR](explanation/parity.qmd) for how numerical fidelity is verified.
